close_reason: Fixed in commit 15ad2ae (move tokio to dev-dependencies in aws-sm)
description:
secretx-aws-sm/Cargo.toml:22 declares 'tokio = { workspace = true }' as a regular [dependencies] entry. The crate's lib code never references tokio directly — only the #[tokio::test] attribute macros in the test module (lib.rs:305, 318, 329, 340, 356) use it.

This forces every downstream non-test consumer of secretx-aws-sm to pull tokio into its build graph for no runtime reason. Tokio brings in mio, parking_lot, and a substantial async runtime; the workspace already sets features = ["rt", "sync", "macros"].

Fix: move the dep to [dev-dependencies]:

[dev-dependencies]
tokio = { workspace = true, features = ["macros", "rt"] }

The aws-config and aws-sdk-secretsmanager crates pull their own tokio runtime via the SDK, so production paths still get a tokio runtime — but downstream consumers gain the freedom to swap to a different async runtime if desired (e.g. tokio with different features, or a future smol-based replacement).

Note: secretx-aws-ssm has the identical anti-pattern (Cargo.toml:20). Filed here for aws-sm; flag aws-ssm in its own pass.
title: secretx-aws-sm: tokio is a regular dependency but used only for #[tokio::test]
